findings: Neither 'collaboration-bus' nor 'proposal-review-improve-consensus' protocol appears anywhere in SKILL.md or README.md — the terms are entirely absent from both files.

The coordination model documented in both files is strictly filesystem-based IPC: atomic file renames (mv) on `.stampede/{run_id}/` directories. README.md line 39 and line 52 describe this as 'filesystem as atomic message bus' — a task-claiming mechanism, not a collaboration or consensus bus.
README.md line 173 explicitly states agents 'didn't even know each other existed', confirming no inter-agent proposal/review/consensus channel is implemented or documented.

No 'SwarmMesh' term appears in either file. The metaswarm architecture (SKILL.md, README.md lines 83, 160) uses nested commander directories and child-agents.jsonl for telemetry, but these are unidirectional write paths (worker → filesystem → monitor), not a bidirectional collaboration bus.
The only 'protocol' named in SKILL.md is the Shadow Score protocol (lines 332, 364) — a quality measurement/scoring construct, not a collaboration or consensus mechanism.
Telemetry in SKILL.md (lines 895–900) is an anonymous opt-in curl ping to an external URL, unrelated to sub-agent collaboration telemetry.

ALIGNMENT GAP: If a collaboration-bus / proposal-review-improve-consensus protocol is intended as part of the metaswarm design, it has zero documented specification or adoption path in either audited file. No creation, no message schema, no consumer definition, no lifecycle management is described.
